title: Writing a Research Proposal - Multiple Response

questions.question: Which of the following belong in a research proposal? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A proposal contains motivation, background/theoretical framework, and the research question (plus hypothesis), along with methods. D and E are excluded because a proposal plans a future study and therefore has neither results nor a discussion of findings.

questions.question: Which sections are present in a paper but NOT in a proposal? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A paper reports an already-conducted study, so it adds Results and Discussion/Conclusion that the proposal lacks. Methods, motivation, and background appear in both, since the proposal is essentially the first half of the paper.

questions.question: Which items are explicitly required by the proposal assignment? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Names of all contributing group members
options.C: Background based on at least five relevant scientific sources

questions.explanation: The assignment requires group member names, an explicit RQ, background grounded in at least five scientific sources, and the attached Technology Statement. D is wrong because a proposal has no results, only planned analyses.

questions.question: For a human-subject experiment, which elements must the methods section specify? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A human-subject experiment must state the groups compared, the between/within design, the DV and IV, and the planned statistical analyses. D belongs to a machine-learning experiment, not a human-subject one.

questions.question: For a machine-learning experiment, which elements must the methods section specify? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A machine-learning experiment must state the dataset, the features to extract, the algorithms compared, and the evaluation measures. E (between/within design) is a human-subject experiment concept, not an ML one.

questions.question: Which are the 5 C's of writing with sources? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The 5 C's are Cite, Compare, Contrast, Critique, and Connect. E ('Collect') is not one of them; the missing genuine C in this list is Contrast.

questions.question: Which uses of AI are RESPONSIBLE under the course's Index Level 3 policy? (Select all that apply)

options.B: Help with coding or analysis only if you already know how
options.C: Using AI to learn, such as asking questions or spotting bugs
options.D: Generating paragraphs of content to paste into your text
options.E: Asking AI to find the scientific sources for you

questions.explanation: Responsible use means AI supports your process: brainstorming, code/analysis help when you can verify it, and learning. D (generating content to submit) and E (finding sources) are forbidden because AI invents facts and hallucinates sources.

questions.question: Which statements about reading a synthesis matrix are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Sources go across the top and main ideas down the side
options.B: Reading horizontally (one idea across sources) shows synthesis
options.C: Reading vertically (one source) produces a summary
options.D: Every box in the matrix must be filled in
options.E: A very full row means the idea is too big and should be split

questions.explanation: Sources sit across the top with ideas down the side; a horizontal read gives synthesis, a vertical read gives a summary, and a very full row means the idea is too big to split. D is false because many boxes legitimately stay empty.

questions.question: Which are the three source-misuse sub-errors named in the section? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Lumping citations at the end of a paragraph
options.D: Citing more than five sources per claim

questions.explanation: The three source-misuse errors are lumping citations at the paragraph's end, claims with no citation, and listing instead of synthesising. D and E are not among the named errors.

questions.question: Which statements about the proposal-to-paper relationship are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: The proposal is essentially the first half of the paper
options.B: A proposal's goal is to convince readers to approve the study
options.C: Methods described in the proposal may change later
options.D: A proposal must report a finished study's contribution
options.E: The proposal and the paper share identical goals

questions.explanation: The proposal is the first half of the paper, aims to get the study approved, and its methods may legitimately change. D is false because the proposal concerns a future study, and E is false because their goals differ: a proposal seeks approval while a paper argues the conducted study was good.
